commit.message:
Fix player session errors: entities forEach guard + WebGPU error screen

Entities TypeError fix (MultiplayerProvider):
- Guard initial entities.forEach with optional chaining + try-catch;
  room.state.entities is not hydrated immediately on joinOrCreate —
  the onAdd listener populates it as server patches arrive

WebGPU browser check (App):
- Add WebGPUGate React error boundary wrapping the whole app
- Checks navigator.gpu before rendering; shows a friendly 'WebGPU required'
  screen with a Chrome download link if unsupported
- Also catches any Three.js render-phase crash (e.g. WebGPU module init
  failure) and shows the same screen instead of a blank crash

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add per-song notes for voters with reveal screen display (#26)

Adds tweet-sized (280 char) notes that any voter can attach to any song,
even after voting closes. Notes are shown on the results reveal screen
as entertaining commentary.

### Database
- New `notes` table (migration 007) with unique constraint on `(game_id,
voterName, country)`
- `deleteGame` cleanup extended to include notes

### Data layer & actions
- `getNotesForVoter`, `upsertNote`, `getAllNotes` in `data.ts`
- `submitNote` server action with server-side length enforcement
- `SongResult` extended with `notes: { voter, note }[]`
- Shared `MAX_NOTE_LENGTH = 280` constant

### Voting UI
- `NoteEditor` client component rendered under each song's rating
buttons
- Pure text "✏️ add note" / "✏️ edit note" button, right-aligned with
`pr-2`, min 24×24px tap target
- Edit mode: 2-row textarea with native `maxLength`, char counter,
Save/Cancel
- Saved notes shown as italic text; editor available regardless of game
closed state

### Results reveal
- "💬 Things said about this song:" section per song card, showing
voter-attributed quoted notes

### Voting page with note buttons and saved note

![vote-page](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/86e7397c-defe-42ff-9581-354606d6a051)

### Note editor open

![note-edit](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b04cb5a-a4e4-4546-8dfa-6f2689c3a9c5)

### Saved note

![note-saved](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1b5d7cd2-615a-4d13-81f3-940edfab3a92)

### Results reveal with notes

![reveal-notes](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/096eaaf5-6529-4a2a-8871-52a3e2d78643)

---------

fix(ci): target Java 21 (LTS) instead of 25 for Jenkins compatibility

Java 25 is not available on the Jenkins agent. Java 21 is LTS,
widely pre-installed, and supports all required features (virtual
threads, records, sealed classes). Remove JDK tool block from
Jenkinsfile since the agent default JDK will be 21+.

fix(#206): replace stuck ENQUEUED workers on Samsung (#209)

* fix(#206): replace stuck ENQUEUED workers on Samsung

ExistingWorkPolicy.KEEP was preventing re-dispatch of WorkManager workers
that got stuck in ENQUEUED state on Samsung due to aggressive battery
optimization. Now uses REPLACE unless a download is genuinely RUNNING,
ensuring stale ENQUEUED jobs are cancelled and replaced with fresh
requests that get dispatched promptly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#206): fix 3 review issues — updateState race, TOCTOU, observer leak

- Move updateState(Downloading()) inside coroutine, conditional on REPLACE policy
  so in-progress download progress isn't reset to 0 when KEEP is chosen
- Move request build + enqueueUniqueWork inside same withContext(IO) block
  to shrink TOCTOU window between WorkInfo snapshot and enqueue call
- Add ConcurrentHashMap<KernelModel, Job> observerJobs + ensureObserving()
  to prevent accumulating observeWorkInfo coroutines on retry-heavy devices

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#206): use compute() in ensureObserving for atomic check-then-act

ConcurrentHashMap.get() + put() is not atomic — two concurrent
startDownload() calls for the same model could both pass the isActive
check and spawn duplicate observer coroutines. compute() holds the
per-key lock across the entire read-check-and-write, making it atomic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tui): scrollable input — unlimited lines with viewport window

- Remove MAX_INPUT_ROWS hard cap from Shift+Enter and paste insertion;
  buffer now stores unlimited lines
- Add scroll_offset field to InputWidget for viewport tracking
- display_lines() returns only the visible viewport slice (5 rows)
- cursor_position() returns viewport-relative row
- ensure_cursor_visible() keeps cursor within viewport on every mutation
- Render ▲/▼ scroll indicators on input right edge when content overflows
- Reset scroll_offset on clear (Ctrl+U), submit, and completion accept
- Eliminate all early returns in handle_key for consistent scroll updates
- Update 2 tests for new unlimited behavior, all 378 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: queue workflow — duplicate prevention, clickable tiles, per-status numbering, check-in time

1. DUPLICATE CHECK-IN PREVENTION (backend)
   - StaffWorkflowService.checkInToQueue: checks for active queue entry for same
     animal today before INSERT — throws DUPLICATE_CHECKIN:<queueNum>:<status>
   - Controller returns HTTP 409 with user-friendly message:
     'This patient is already in the queue as #2 (in triage). Discharge or mark
     no-show before checking in again.'
   - Frontend handleCheckIn: catches error, displays in red banner at top of modal
   - No more duplicate Buddy #1 / #2 same patient same day

2. CLICKABLE STAT TILES (filter queue)
   - Waiting / In Triage / In Exam / In Treatment / Emergencies tiles are now
     clickable — click sets queueStatusFilter, queue list updates immediately
   - Active tile fills with its status color (inverted: white text on colored bg)
   - Click same tile again to clear filter (toggle behavior)
   - Avg Wait and Today Total are non-clickable (no meaningful filter)
   - Active filter shown as dismissible chip next to 'Patient Queue' heading

3. PER-STATUS QUEUE NUMBERS
   - When a status tile is active, each row shows position within that filtered
     group (1, 2, 3…) as primary number — easy navigation within status
   - Global queue number (Q#N) shown as smaller secondary reference below
   - When no filter active, global queue_number shown as before

4. CHECK-IN TIME ON EACH ROW
   - Each queue row now shows: 🕐 Checked in: <formatted datetime>
   - Uses formatDateTime() from useSettings() — respects admin date/time format

5. CHECK-IN MODAL UX (standards compliance)
   - ✕ close button + overlay-click-to-close
   - Required field * marker + amber helper text when no animal selected
   - Optional labels on reason and priority fields
   - Error banner at top for API errors including duplicate detection
   - Loading state: '⏳ Checking in...' during submission

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): move JDK tool to Test stage only to avoid pipeline startup failure

tools{} at pipeline level fails to start if the tool is not yet
configured in Jenkins. Moving it to stage level means only the
Test stage fails (gracefully) until JDK-25 is configured.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix token placement: wire canvas click-to-place and offline mode support

Root cause: EntityLayer's handleCanvasClick only sent movement messages,
never placed new tokens. TokenToolPanel used local React state that EntityLayer
couldn't read. Offline mode had room=null so room?.send() was a no-op.

Changes:
- useMultiplayerStore: add pendingToken state (name, type, movementRange),
  setPendingToken(), and placeToken(cellX, cellZ) action; placeToken works both
  online (room.send + optimistic local add) and offline (local store only)
- TokenToolPanel: remove local useState; bind name/type inputs to pendingToken
  in the store via setPendingToken; handle remove/toggleVisible/patch offline too
- EntityLayer: import placeToken + paintedCells; on canvas click with no
  entity selected → placeToken at clicked cell (only on painted floor cells);
  drag pointerUp updates entity position locally when offline
- Remove unused useState import from TokenToolPanel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: increase FunctionGemma MAX_TOKENS 256→1024 to match ekv1024 model (#207)

The model file is the ekv1024 variant (1024-token KV cache) but the session
was created with maxNumTokens=256, causing every inference call to fail with
'Input token ids are too long: 319 >= 256'. System prompt + skill declarations
alone exceed 256 tokens before the user message is even appended.

commit.message:
fix: triage modal — remove contradictory level+priority dual input, unify into single selector

Root cause: triageLevel (1-5 numeric) and priority (emergency/urgent/high/normal/low dropdown)
were the SAME concept displayed twice, allowing contradictory combinations like
'Level 5 (Minor) + Emergency priority'.

Changes:
- Added TRIAGE_LEVELS map: 1→emergency/Critical, 2→urgent/Urgent, 3→high/High,
  4→normal/Moderate, 5→low/Minor — each with color, bg, icon, description
- Removed priority field from triageForm (was a separate state that could contradict level)
- handleTriage: auto-derives priority from triageLevel via TRIAGE_LEVELS map
- Triage modal redesigned: 5 color-coded row buttons (number + label + description in one)
  replacing the separate number buttons + priority dropdown
- Added auto-derived priority badge below selector confirming the assigned priority
- Added ✕ close button + overlay-click-to-close
- Added optional label on notes field
- Kept PRIORITIES const (still used by check-in, new case, referral forms)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci(github): add container support for self-hosted runners (#9)

* ci(github): add container support for self-hosted runners

Switch all workflows to use containers on self-hosted runners for improved
consistency and isolation. Add system dependency installation steps and
configure Docker and Git safe directory where needed. Update job dependencies
and permissions for better security and workflow reliability. This enables
better compatibility with shared self-hosted environments.

chore(ci): use public-repo runner in all workflows

Replace the custom self-hosted runner labels with [public-repo] in all
GitHub Actions workflow files. This change standardizes the runner
environment and improves compatibility for public repositories.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

fix the core volume mount problem

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): update wp-env test config and add --update flag

- Remove "plugins" field from .wp-env.test.json to prevent broken mounts
- Add dynamic host mapping for plugin in test config
- Add --update flag to npm run wp-env:test start commands for fresh env

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: improve Docker mapping and plugin activation

Update workflows to fix wp-env host mapping, add plugin activation steps,
and ensure Docker environments are properly started and stopped. Switch
PHPUnit commands to use tests-cli for accurate environment handling.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: unify env vars and ports for self-hosted runners

Refactor GitHub Actions workflows to standardize environment variable
usage and port assignments for wp-env across build, E2E, and PHPUnit
jobs. Removes dynamic COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME generation and sets static
WP_ENV_PORT and WP_ENV_TESTS_PORT values to avoid conflicts on shared
self-hosted runners. Updates .wp-env.test.json to include testsPort.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): add --yes flag to wp-env destroy commands

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* fixed the --yes of wp-env destroy with --force

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* added a pause for indepth analysis

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* disabled spcific section to be able to check the workflow

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: set WP_ENV_HOME for isolated wp-env data

Ensure wp-env uses a dedicated /tmp/wp-env directory for all jobs by
setting WP_ENV_HOME and mounting it in the container. This prevents
permission issues and data conflicts in CI. Updates all workflow steps
to use the new environment variable.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(github): increase timeouts and fix safe.directory

- Increase `timeout-minutes` for build, e2e, and phpunit workflows to 30
  minutes for more reliable CI runs.
- Set `timeout_minutes` to 15 for Docker environment startup steps.
- Add `git config --global --add safe.directory '/tmp/wp-env/*'` to
  address Git safe directory issues in Docker.
- Run E2E tests with `--debug` for better diagnostics.
- No functional changes to plugin code.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ci): use dynamic outputs for job filtering

Update CI workflow to use step outputs for job filtering instead of hardcoded
values. This enables more flexible and accurate job execution based on file
changes.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix permission denied errors for test artifacts

When running in the Docker-out-of-Docker CI setup, the actions/checkout
step creates the workspace files as the `root` user. However, wp-env
dynamically configures its internal test containers to run as `wpuser`
(UID 1001) to match the host user executing `wp-env start`.

This caused a permission collision where the test runner (running as UID
1001) could not write test artifacts (like .phpunit.result.cache and code
coverage XML/HTML reports) back to the workspace directory owned by root.

This adds a step to `chown -R wpuser:wpuser .` across the build, e2e,
and phpunit workflows immediately before starting wp-env. This ensures the
containerized test runner has the necessary write privileges to generate
and upload coverage reports successfully.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* dbg: e2e test suite

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(e2e): path for the npx

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(e2e): giving it the wp-env test one

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(e2e): disable Playwright webServer and clean env usage

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ci): set WP_BASE_URL for E2E tests in workflow

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(e2e): support host.docker.internal in e2e workflow

Add --add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway to container options in
the reusable-e2e.yml workflow. Update WP_BASE_URL to use
host.docker.internal for E2E tests, enabling proper connectivity between
Playwright and the WordPress instance when running in Docker.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ci): improve Playwright network reliability in e2e workflow

Use --network=host for the CI container to ensure Playwright shares the
host Docker daemon's network. This prevents "ECONNREFUSED" errors when
WordPress issues 302 redirects to "localhost", avoiding loopback traps
inside the CI container. Also remove WP_BASE_URL env from test command.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ci): enhance E2E reliability with socat proxy

Switch CI container from --network=host to --add-host and socat proxy for
localhost:8889. This ensures Playwright and WordPress agree on "localhost"
and prevents network isolation issues. Adds socat to dependencies and
updates E2E test step to forward traffic, improving test stability.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(test): enable headless mode in Playwright config

Set Playwright to run in headless mode by default for more consistent and
reliable test execution in CI and local environments. This avoids issues
with UI rendering and ensures tests do not require a display server.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(e2e): remove debug flag and extend base config

Removed the --debug flag from the E2E test command in the CI workflow to
ensure tests run in standard mode. Updated Playwright config to spread
baseConfig.use for better configuration inheritance. These changes
improve consistency and maintainability of E2E test execution.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ci): enable dynamic outputs and e2e job triggers

Updated CI workflow to use dynamic outputs from filter steps instead of
hardcoded values. Enabled e2e job to trigger based on detection results
and phpunit status. Removed outdated comments for clarity.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestion from @dipankardas011

* ci: improve CI blueprint and Playwright config

- Switch blueprint JSON mutation from Node.js to jq in the workflow,
  simplifying dependencies and improving readability.
- Add jq to system dependencies in the workflow.
- Refactor Playwright config to disable webServer orchestration by
  destructuring base config, preventing port conflicts and aligning with
  manual wp-env lifecycle management.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: update workflow dependencies and permissions

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(playground): add GitHub CLI install to PR preview workflow

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: simplify workflows and update test commands

- Use `ubuntu-latest` instead of custom container in CI workflow
- Remove unnecessary system dependency and git safe directory steps
- Use `sudo -E -u wpuser` for environment consistency in reusable workflows
- Remove explicit port settings from `.wp-env.test.json`
- Update `test:php` script to use `cli` instead of `tests-cli`
- Improve workflow maintainability and reduce complexity

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(github): fix PATH for sudo -u wpuser npm commands

Ensure PATH is preserved when running npm and npx commands as wpuser
via sudo in GitHub Actions workflows. This prevents issues where
npm, npx, or node may not be found due to a restricted environment
PATH. Updates all relevant commands in reusable-build, reusable-e2e,
and reusable-phpunit workflows.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "ci(github): fix PATH for sudo -u wpuser npm commands"

This reverts commit cafed50944a4d1e9586fce45160223dea06200e8.

* ci(github): add npm to PATH for wpuser in workflows

Ensure npm is available in PATH for the wpuser by updating .bashrc in
build, e2e, and phpunit reusable workflows. This helps prevent issues
with npm commands not being found during CI runs.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* reverted the sudo -E to bring the system back to stability

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: restore public workflows

* ci: Remove unnecessary release-public.yml

* ci: docs and cleanup

* ci: Fix run id

* ci: trigger on workflow changes

* ci: stop using TESTS_PORT (first pass)

* ci: remove activate plugin step

* ci: more wp-env 11 fixes

* ci(private): added the CI playground preview system deps package

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix *-public.yml references

* ci: use use ramsey/composer-install@3.2.1 for private builds

* ci(github): update workflow for self-hosted runner

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ci): add Docker Compose setup to build workflow

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): switch to actions-runner container, drop wpuser

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): install GitHub CLI in PR preview workflow

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): add sudo to privileged commands in workflows

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): add post-run cleanup to reusable workflows

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): improve post-run cleanup in reusable workflows

Enhance post-run cleanup steps in all reusable GitHub Actions workflows to
remove artifacts from both /__w and $HOME directories. This prevents
pollution between workflow runs and ensures a cleaner environment for
subsequent jobs. Updates the cleanup step name for clarity.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): improve cleanup to prevent workspace pollution

Enhances the post-run cleanup steps in reusable GitHub Actions workflows.
Now uses `sudo` to ensure all files, including hidden ones, are removed from
the workspace, home, and workflow directories. Also selectively deletes
contents in /__w/_temp/ while preserving locked mount points. This prevents
artifact and environment pollution between workflow runs.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): use actions-runner container for workflows

Switch Jest and JS lint workflows to use the ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:2.333.0
container image instead of node:22. This aligns the CI environment with GitHub
Actions runner standards and may improve compatibility and consistency across jobs.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): clean wp-env and playwright-cache in workflows

Add steps to reusable GitHub Actions workflows to remove contents of
/tmp/wp-env in build, e2e, and phpunit jobs, and /tmp/playwright-cache in
e2e jobs. This ensures a clean environment for each workflow run and
prevents leftover files from affecting subsequent jobs.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): update ACL setup for GitHub Actions

Expand setfacl permissions to additional directories in reusable
workflows. This ensures runner access to /github/home, /github/workflow,
and /tmp/wp-env (plus /tmp/playwright-cache for e2e). Removes redundant
commands and streamlines Docker group setup.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): expand setfacl paths for compatibility

Replaced brace expansion in setfacl commands with explicit paths in GitHub
Actions workflows. This improves compatibility with environments where
brace expansion is not supported, ensuring correct permissions are set.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: update Docker setup in CI workflows

- Removed redundant Docker group permission steps from e2e workflow
- Added Docker Compose setup to phpunit workflow for consistency
- Ensured proper environment configuration for CI jobs

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(workflows): use volumes instead of options for container

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* trying to fix the composer install with working-dir

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): improve cleanup and composer install steps

Update reusable GitHub Actions workflows to:
- Use current working directory for cleanup to avoid accidental deletion
- Remove redundant composer-install working-directory input

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): simplify workspace cleanup in workflows

Refactored the cleanup steps in multiple GitHub Actions workflows to use a
single `sudo rm -rf ./* .[!.]*` command. This change removes both regular and
hidden files more concisely, improving readability and maintainability of the
CI scripts.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update .github/workflows/README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): make cleanup steps resilient to missing files

Add `|| true` to `rm -rf` commands in reusable GitHub Actions workflows to
prevent failures when target files or directories do not exist. This improves
workflow robustness and avoids unnecessary errors during cleanup steps.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): remove redundant safe.directory config

Removes the step that added '/tmp/wp-env/*' to git's safe.directory list
from reusable workflow files.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(github): pin actions-runner image by digest

Pin the GitHub Actions runner container image to a specific SHA256 digest
across all reusable workflow files for improved security and reproducibility.
Also refactor E2E workflow to check port usage and verify socat startup
before running tests.

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>

* unused section

Signed-off-by: Dipankar Das <65275144+dipankardas011@users.noreply.github.com>
